incentive.statement: A choice between one result and a full result paradigm belongs to the Result surface, uses one typed vocabulary, and cannot let malformed or hidden state alter application authorization.

coordinator.responsibility: Coordinate the typed scope contract, shell and Canvas parity, application authorization, URL continuity, focused proof, and alignment.

outcome: Both NNC and VNC use one fail-closed single/paradigm selection contract while retaining separate paradigm dimensions, Result ownership, and Canvas/Andrews-only visible evidence.

responsibilities.statement: Keep the canonical controls in Result and derive their exact ordered values and tags from the typed output-scope contract.

actors.contribution: Preserve Result ownership and replace handwritten scope inventories with contract-derived shell records.

responsibilities.statement: Make VNC application and NNC/VNC Result projection consume recognized typed scope frames; invalid explicit or hidden state must not authorize output.

actors.contribution: Centralize scope recognition and preserve the distinct NNC and VNC enumeration and authorization rules.

responsibilities.statement: Persist literal canonical scope for NNC and Direct/derived VNC, while retaining compatibility with the existing derived positional capsule and rejecting malformed explicit values.

actors.contribution: Close Direct-VNC persistence loss and stale malformed-capsule behavior without reinterpreting existing entrada/v1 capsule positions.

responsibilities.statement: Prove exact inventory parity, invalid-scope blocking, active-only VNC paradigm behavior, hostile URL restoration, and live refresh continuity.

actors.contribution: Add hostile cross-layer authorization, stale-state, persistence, and parity checks.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded target and closure evidence.

actors.contribution: Keep UI ownership separate from grammar authorization.

contracts.invariant: Absent scope defaults to single; recognized single or paradigm is retained; malformed explicit scope is blocked and cannot inherit hidden DOM or alter grammar authorization.

retirements.removalEvidence: Focused shell assertions and fresh browser proof show the VNC scope control in #3 Result and absent from #2 Authority; the same shell contract covers NNC.

closure.detail: Handwritten scope vocabularies, authority-panel ownership, malformed fallback authorization, hidden-DOM inheritance, and VNC URL scope loss are removed from the deciding path.

evidence.detail: Focused runs passed VNC 214/214, NNC 458/458, state 413/413, UI 413/413, registry 43/43, derivation 224/224, orthography 41/41, and runtime 9/9; syntax and residual-duplicate scans passed.

Fresh localhost entry 053 restored VNC and NNC paradigm URLs, retained single on the inactive role, rejected a fabricated VNC scope to the clean default URL and single, and reported zero warning/error logs. Populated VNC full-paradigm generation remains covered by focused tests because it exceeded the in-app verification tab resource boundary.

Architecture metrics stayed at baseline: responsibility/actor 0, renderer construction 38, optional renderer dependency 0, registered-contract coverage 200, and browser scripts 1. The repository gate remains red for the inherited 607-line live board and cross-action dirty-worktree overlap, not for an output-scope-attributable regression.
